title: vicia peregrina subsp. michauxii
intro: Vicia peregrina subsp. michauxii, also known as Michaux's vetchling, is a perennial legume that belongs to the family Fabaceae and is native to North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems. Vicia peregrina subsp. michauxii can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, although its efficacy and safety need further scientific validation.
